📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Eurasia Review published a comprehensive analysis in December 2025 finding “almost unanimous opinion” among analysts that Malaysia’s race-based economic policies — rooted in the New Economic Policy (NEP) of 1971 — have failed their “overarching goal of national unity.” Yet the analysis concluded the government is “highly unlikely to abandon” them. The NEP and its successors (NDP, NVP, and now Anwar’s various economic frameworks) maintain Bumiputera preferences in government contracts, education, housing, and corporate equity. Despite 50+ years of these policies, Malaysia remains deeply divided along ethnic lines, brain drain continues at alarming rates, and economic inequality persists within the Bumiputera community itself.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The NEP and its successors have been the third rail of Malaysian politics for over 50 years. Every PM — including the “reformist” one — refuses to touch them. When virtually every independent analyst agrees a policy has failed its stated objective, and the government still won’t reform it, the policy exists to serve political interests, not national ones. Anwar’s multiracial “Malaysia Madani” brand is fundamentally incompatible with race-based economic preferences — but admitting that would cost Malay votes. So the contradiction persists and the country pays the price in brain drain, investment distortions, and communal resentment.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The NEP was designed as a 20-year policy (1971-1990). It’s now in its 55th year. The Bumiputera corporate equity target of 30% has been a moving goalpost for decades — with the government periodically changing the methodology to show either progress or continued need, depending on what serves the political moment. Meanwhile, the primary beneficiaries of Bumiputera preferences have been politically connected elites, not ordinary Malays. The wealth gap within the Bumiputera community is enormous. Race-based policies designed to help poor Malays have been captured by rich, connected Malays — and PH, like every government before it, refuses to acknowledge this because the fiction of universal Bumiputera benefit is essential to electoral politics.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Mahathir introduced the Vision 2020 framework that was supposed to move Malaysia beyond race-based economics. It didn’t. Abdullah Badawi promised the NDP would be more “merit-based.” It wasn’t. Najib’s NTP made similar promises. Anwar’s frameworks use different acronyms for the same structure. The NEP is unkillable not because it works, but because dismantling it would require telling millions of Malay voters that the preference system their political identity is built on needs to change. No politician has the courage to do this — least of all one who needs Malay votes to survive.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Analysts — your own think tanks included — say unanimously that race-based economic policies have failed their stated goal of national unity. Brain drain accelerates as non-Bumiputera professionals leave for countries where they’re judged on merit. Investment distortions persist. Communal resentment deepens. And your response is to maintain the policies unchanged because dismantling them would cost votes. “Malaysia Madani” cannot coexist with race-based economic preferences. Choose one. Or admit that “Madani” was always just a slogan — not a policy direction.

Sources: Eurasia Review

item.title: Penang's Planning Failures Exposed — Traffic, Floods, Vanishing Green Spaces After 16 Years of DAP
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow / Penang Forum / Aliran

Category: Social

Date: 2025-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2025, Aliran published a comprehensive report documenting why ‘Penang’s development planning is failing its people.’ Residents demanded transparency as traffic congestion, floods, and vanishing green spaces exposed ‘systematic failures in planning’ under the DAP-led state government. The report detailed how development approvals continued without adequate infrastructure, environmental assessments were inadequate, and community input was sidelined. Penang Forum — a coalition of civil society groups — has been documenting these failures for years.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This isn’t opposition propaganda — it’s civil society documentation. Aliran, Penang Forum, SAM, and CAP are independent organisations with decades of credibility. Their consistent finding is that DAP governs Penang like a developer-state: approving projects without adequate infrastructure, ignoring environmental impact, and sidelining community opposition. After 16 years of unbroken DAP rule, these failures are entirely DAP’s responsibility.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP Youth’s response to traffic criticism was to blame PN — claiming transport projects were ‘delayed during PN’s time.’ PN governed federally for less than two years. DAP has governed Penang for 16 years. Blaming 22 months of PN governance for 16 years of traffic planning failures is not a rebuttal — it’s a confession that DAP has nothing better to say. The development approvals that created traffic chaos were signed by DAP-appointed officials.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Penang’s development trajectory under DAP has been consistently criticised: the PSI reclamation destroying fishermen’s livelihoods, the undersea tunnel going nowhere, hillside development causing floods, and George Town’s heritage zone under pressure from commercial interests. Each issue is documented by multiple independent organisations. The pattern is clear and consistent.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Independent civil society organisations with decades of credibility are documenting systematic governance failures in your state. Your response is to blame PN for traffic. After 16 years, you own every planning failure in Penang. Commission an independent planning audit. Publish development approval data. Implement a moratorium on hillside development. Or keep blaming others while the traffic gets worse and the green spaces disappear.

Sources: Aliran | FMT | FMT

item.title: Amirudin Rolls Out Flood Recovery Plan — After Selangor's Worst Floods Under His Watch
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2025-11-29

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 29 November 2025, Amirudin rolled out a ‘major mobilisation’ for post-flood recovery as evacuee numbers surged across Selangor. Water from northern Selangor flooded into Kuala Langat and Sepang. JPS installed four pumps in Kuala Selangor with six more planned. A police officer went missing after a car skidded into a storm drain near KLIA during the floods. The Met Department withdrew severe rain warnings, but damage was extensive.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Selangor experiences catastrophic flooding almost every year. The December 2021 floods killed dozens and displaced hundreds of thousands. Amirudin promised improved mitigation — RM billions in flood infrastructure, better early warning systems, and drainage upgrades. Four years later, evacuees are still being mobilised, pumps are still being installed reactively, and people are still losing their lives. The flood response is reactive, not preventive.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Post-flood recovery is not flood management — it’s disaster response to a management failure. Installing pumps after the water arrives is not mitigation. The question Amirudin never answers: where are the flood retention ponds, upgraded drainage systems, and development controls that would prevent flooding in the first place? Selangor continues to approve hillside and lowland development that worsens flood risk. The state profits from development approvals, then spends public money on cleanup.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: The December 2021 Shah Alam floods, the 2022 Taman Sri Muda disaster, the 2023 Klang floods — Selangor floods are now annual events. Each time, Amirudin announces mobilisation, recovery plans, and future improvements. Each year, the same areas flood again. The definition of insanity applies.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Stop announcing reactive flood responses as if they’re achievements. A pump installed after the flood is a failure, not a success. Publish Selangor’s flood mitigation capital expenditure vs. development approval revenue for the last 7 years. Show the rakyat whether the state is spending more on preventing floods or profiting from the development that causes them.

Sources: Malay Mail

item.title: Social Media Ban for Under-16s — Enforcement Plan: TBD
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2025-11-24

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil announced in November 2025 that Malaysia would ban social media access for children under 16, following Australia’s lead. The ban was set for implementation in 2026. However, the NYT noted the announcement was “light on details” — no age verification infrastructure exists, no enforcement mechanism was outlined, and no timeline for implementation was provided. The government framed it as protecting children from online harms, but offered no technical plan for making it work.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A social media ban for under-16s sounds decisive. But without enforcement infrastructure, it’s a headline, not a policy. Age verification online is technically difficult — even Australia, which passed its ban first, has struggled with implementation. Malaysia has neither the digital infrastructure, the regulatory capacity, nor the tech industry cooperation to enforce such a ban. Meanwhile, the announcement distracts from the government’s own record of using social media regulations to suppress dissent — including the arrest of FMT journalist Rex Tan and the expansion of MCMC’s content moderation powers.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government that can’t enforce basic traffic laws, stop illegal waste dumping, or verify the integrity of its own procurement processes is going to verify the age of every social media user in Malaysia? This is performative governance at its worst. Fahmi announced a ban with no enforcement plan — because the plan doesn’t exist. It cannot exist without massive investment in digital ID infrastructure, cooperation from global tech platforms, and privacy safeguards that Malaysia has never implemented. The announcement was designed to generate a positive news cycle, not to protect children.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia has a long history of announcing tech regulations without enforcement mechanisms. The Anti-Fake News Act was passed and repealed. MCMC’s licensing regime for social media platforms was announced with fanfare but implementation details remain vague. The Online Safety Bill has been in discussion for years. Each announcement generates headlines; none generates meaningful change. Meanwhile, Malaysia’s youth screen time is among the highest in the world, and the government’s response is a ban it cannot enforce rather than investment in digital literacy it could actually deliver.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You announced a ban with no enforcement plan. That’s not policy — it’s a press release. If you’re serious about protecting children online, invest in digital literacy programmes in schools. Fund parental education on screen time management. Build the age verification infrastructure before announcing the ban, not after. Or admit that this was a headline-grabbing announcement designed to match Australia’s news cycle, with no intention of following through.

Sources: NYT | CNA | Malay Mail

item.title: Farhash Tries to GAG MalaysiaNow — Courts Reject Injunctions THREE Times
item.description:
Said by: Farhash Wafa Salvador / Courts

Category: Social

Date: 2025-10-12

Farhash filed three attempts to prevent MalaysiaNow from publishing Sabah mining scandal reports. Shah Alam High Court and Court of Appeal both rejected the injunctions. The PM’s former aide tried to gag the press three times and failed three times.

item.title: Anthony Loke Admits Malaysian Society Is 'Polarised' — But Blames Opposition for Playing Up Race
item.description:
Said by: Anthony Loke, Transport Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2025-09-11

Polarised, But Don’t Blame Us

In September 2025, Transport Minister Anthony Loke told Malaysians in Hong Kong that society had become “more polarised” with intensifying race rhetoric. His diagnosis was accurate. His prescription — blame the opposition — was intellectually dishonest.

The Facts

Fact: At an event in Hong Kong, Loke acknowledged that Malaysia was increasingly divided along racial, class, and education lines, with social media amplifying hate speech and misinformation.

Fact: He attributed the polarisation primarily to “the opposition playing up such issues for political capital” — as though the government bore no responsibility for the fractures.

Fact: This came from a government that reshuffled its cabinet along racial lines in December 2025, giving UMNO more positions to shore up Malay support while reducing DAP’s presence.

Fact: JAKIM’s proposed interfaith guidelines in 2024 — which restricted how non-Muslims could engage in religious dialogue — divided communities and were widely criticised by civil society groups as discriminatory.

Fact: The government has made no move to reform race-based affirmative action policies (NEP-era quotas in education, procurement, and housing), despite PH’s manifesto promising a “needs-based” approach.

Fact: Anwar’s government has relied heavily on racial and religious rhetoric when convenient — including Anwar’s own Malay-first speeches at UMNO general assemblies, his defence of Malay-Muslim “special rights,” and his coalition’s use of the Sedition Act against those who question racial policies.

Fact: The SCMP report noted that despite Loke’s optimism about “overcoming challenges,” Malaysia’s racial polarisation metrics — measured by survey data on inter-ethnic trust — have worsened since 2023.

Why It Matters

  • Admitting the country is polarised while blaming only the opposition is the political equivalent of diagnosing the disease and prescribing someone else’s medicine. The government controls policy, appointments, media regulation, and the machinery of state. If polarisation is worsening on your watch, you own it.
  • The government’s own actions have actively contributed to division: a cabinet reshuffle that prioritised racial coalition management over competence, interfaith guidelines that alienated minorities, continued race-based policies that PH promised to reform, and a PM who code-switches between reform rhetoric for international audiences and ethno-religious rhetoric for domestic ones.
  • Loke made these remarks in Hong Kong — not in Malaysia. Malaysian politicians are remarkably candid about domestic failures when speaking to foreign audiences. At home, the same problems are denied or deflected. The gap between what they say abroad and what they do domestically is the real measure of governance.
  • The opposition absolutely plays the race card. PN’s ethno-religious populism is real and dangerous. But blaming PN for polarisation while your own government maintains race-based policies, panders to religious conservatives, and uses sedition laws against those who challenge racial orthodoxy is not analysis — it is deflection.
  • If even a DAP minister — from the party that was supposed to represent multiracial reform — cannot honestly diagnose why Malaysia is polarised, then PH has fully absorbed the BN playbook: acknowledge the symptom, blame the enemy, change nothing. *Source: *SCMP

item.title: Penang South Islands Reclamation — Destroying 4,000 Fishermen's Livelihoods for a Developer's Dream
item.description:
Said by: Chow Kon Yeow, Penang Chief Minister / DAP

Category: Social

Date: 2025-08-20

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: The Penang South Islands (PSI) reclamation project — now rebranded as ‘Silicon Island’ — is the DAP-led state government’s mega-development plan to create 930 hectares of artificial land off Penang’s southern coast. As of August 2025, 80 hectares had been reclaimed. The project was originally three islands, later downsized to one after public outcry. More than 4,000 coastal fishermen are affected. Only RM11.7 million has been paid to 791 fishermen under the Social Impact Management Plan, and 118 of 295 active fishermen received new boats and engines worth RM10 million. Fishermen lost their court challenge to the Environmental Impact Assessment approval.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: This is the largest land reclamation project in Penang’s history, and it’s being pushed through by DAP — the party that positions itself as pro-people and pro-environment. Academic research confirms the project causes ‘permanent and irreversible loss to the ecosystem.’ Fishermen who have worked these waters for generations are being displaced with minimal compensation. The project is designed to extend Bayan Lepas Free Industrial Zone for semiconductor manufacturing — a corporate objective funded by destroying a community’s livelihood.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: DAP in opposition would have led protests against this. A BN state government reclaiming 930 hectares of sea, displacing thousands of fishermen, and losing court cases over environmental damage would have been DAP’s dream campaign issue. But when DAP does it? It’s ‘development for the people.’ The fishermen who lost their livelihoods are not ’the people’ in DAP’s calculation — they’re collateral damage for FDI numbers.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Penang has a long history of reclamation — Gurney Drive, Tanjung Tokong — but nothing on this scale. Civil society groups including SAM and Penang Forum have documented environmental destruction and community displacement. Fishermen and NGOs were ‘dismayed’ when their court challenge failed. The pattern is clear: DAP governs Penang like a developer-state, prioritising investment over communities.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: 4,000 fishermen displaced. RM11.7 million in compensation for 791 of them — that’s RM14,800 each for losing a livelihood. 930 hectares of seabed destroyed. And you call this development? This is extraction — taking from the sea, taking from the fishermen, and giving to semiconductor companies. If this is PH’s model of governance, the fishermen of Penang now know exactly how much their lives are worth to DAP.

Sources: FMT | Al Jazeera | Aliran

item.title: Toda Peace Institute: Anwar 'Making Race-Based Policies WORSE, Not Better' — Trying to Out-Islamise the Islamists
item.description:
Said by: Toda Peace Institute Analysis

Category: Social

Date: 2025-08-01

📌 What Happened

The Toda Peace Institute published a comprehensive analysis titled ‘The Many Wars of Malaysia’s Anwar Ibrahim,’ concluding that Anwar ‘isn’t just reluctant to disturb the status quo; he is in fact making it worse by trying to out-Islamize the Islamists with gestures, policies and statements that the minorities find deeply worrying.’

🔥 Why It Matters

This is an international peace research institute saying that Malaysia’s supposedly reformist PM is WORSENING racial discrimination, not reducing it. The institutionalised system of race-based policies — described as ‘killing off merit, stifling competition and innovation, breeding minority resentments, and spawning cronyism by creating a rich Malay elite without doing much for poor Malays’ — is being strengthened rather than reformed under Anwar.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Anwar’s Reformasi movement explicitly ‘sought an end to this system of institutionalized racial discrimination.’ That was the CORE PROMISE. Not a peripheral commitment — the fundamental reason minorities supported him. His ascent ‘understandably triggered hopes of racial justice.’ Instead, he’s competing with PAS on who can be more Malay-Muslim, while minorities who voted PH in droves get empty rhetoric and photo ops with the occasional Chinese school allocation announcement.

📜 Previous Cases

In April 2025, when a UiTM professor suggested opening cardiothoracic surgery training to non-Bumiputera students (because people were dying from surgeon shortages), the government was paralysed. The mandatory Malay language requirement for foreign workers was imposed. Bumiputera policies were declared ’non-negotiable’ in US trade talks. ICERD remains unratified. The civil service remains 90% Bumiputera.

🎯 PH Callout

You asked non-Malay Malaysians to trust you. They did — they voted for PH in overwhelming numbers. In return, you’ve not only maintained every discriminatory policy but, according to independent analysis, made things WORSE. You’ve traded minority rights for Malay-Muslim votes. Every minority Malaysian who voted PH deserves an apology — and an explanation for why their trust was weaponised against their own interests.

Sources

  • Toda Peace Institute — Report 184
  • CSIS — The Death of Reformasi

item.title: Freedom House: Malaysia 'Partly Free' — Internet Controls Expanding Under PH
item.description:
Said by: Freedom House / MCMC

Category: Social

Date: 2025-06-01

Freedom House rated Malaysia ‘Partly Free’ in 2025. MCMC continues removing content critical of government. Online Safety Act and CMA amendments gave regulators sweeping censorship powers without independent oversight.

item.title: Human Rights Watch: 'Rights Backslide Under Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim'
item.description:
Said by: Human Rights Watch

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-16

The Reformist Who Backslid on Rights

In January 2025, Human Rights Watch issued a damning assessment: “Rights Backslide Under Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim.” CIVICUS rated Malaysia’s civic space as “obstructed.”

The Facts

Fact: HRW’s Asia researcher Shayna Bauchner said: “In his first year in office, Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim betrayed the reform pledges he made to Malaysian voters so he could hold his coalition together.”

Fact: CIVICUS reported that restrictive laws — especially the CMA and the Sedition Act — continued to be used to silence online dissent and harass human rights defenders.

Fact: 90 arrests were made under the Sedition Act between 2024 and 2025.

Fact: Malaysia has yet to ratify the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR) — a PH promise.

Fact: The government expanded powers under the Sedition Act, CMA, and passed a new Cyber Security Act.

Why It Matters

  • This is not opposition propaganda. This is Human Rights Watch and CIVICUS — independent international organisations.
  • Anwar spent decades as a political prisoner. Now 90 people have been arrested under the same type of law.
  • “Betrayed the reform pledges to hold his coalition together” — that single sentence is the epitaph of the Anwar era. *Sources: HRW · *CIVICUS

item.title: Gambling Addiction Crisis — 4D, Toto, and Online Betting Destroy Families While Government Collects Revenue
item.description:
Said by: Health Ministry / NGOs

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia faces a growing gambling addiction crisis driven by legal 4D and Toto outlets, sports betting, and an explosion of illegal online gambling platforms. The government profits from gambling through licensing fees and gaming taxes — collecting billions annually from licensed operators. Meanwhile, gambling addiction destroys lower-income families, with treatment services remaining minimal compared to the scale of the problem. Online gambling has exploded beyond MCMC’s enforcement capacity, with estimates suggesting billions of ringgit flow through illegal platforms annually.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Gambling addiction disproportionately affects B40 families who can least afford losses. The social costs — family breakdown, debt, crime, mental health deterioration — are borne by communities while the government collects revenue. Licensed gambling operators contribute to political campaigns and enjoy regulatory protection. The government has a financial incentive to maintain gambling revenue and a social responsibility to address addiction. It consistently chooses revenue over responsibility.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: The government simultaneously profits from gambling and claims to combat it. Licensing fees and gaming taxes generate billions for the Treasury. Online gambling enforcement is performative — MCMC blocks a few sites while thousands more operate freely through VPNs and messaging apps. Treatment services for gambling addiction are chronically underfunded. The conflict of interest is structural: the government is both regulator and beneficiary of the industry it’s supposed to control. That’s not governance — it’s a revenue addiction masquerading as regulation.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Gambling regulation in Malaysia has always been politically convenient. Under BN, licensed operators flourished with political connections. Under PH 1.0, no changes were made. Under PH 2.0, the same operators continue with the same political connections, and online gambling has grown unchecked. Each government collects the revenue, underfunds treatment, and claims to be fighting the problem. The Forest City SFZ’s potential for gaming activities has raised further concerns about the government’s relationship with gambling revenue.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You collect billions from gambling while addiction destroys families. Your enforcement against online gambling is performative. Your treatment services are underfunded. The conflict of interest is obvious: you profit from the industry you’re supposed to regulate. Either significantly increase gambling addiction treatment funding — proportional to the revenue you collect — or admit that gambling regulation is a revenue operation, not a public health programme.

Sources: Bernama | FMT | Health Ministry

item.title: Gender Pay Gap Persists at 6-9% — Women Do More Work for Less Pay, Government Does Nothing
item.description:
Said by: DOSM / Talentcorp Data

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

Despite Anwar’s government having prominent women in cabinet and occasional rhetoric about gender equality, Malaysia’s gender pay gap remains stubbornly persistent at an estimated 6-9% across sectors, with significantly larger gaps in certain industries. Women bear a disproportionate burden of unpaid care work, face career penalties for motherhood, and are underrepresented in senior management and board positions.

🔥 Why It Matters

The female labour force participation rate, while improving, still lags regional peers. Maternity protection remains inadequate for gig economy workers. Sexual harassment laws, while strengthened, face enforcement challenges.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

The government has not introduced pay transparency legislation, gender-disaggregated budgeting, or mandatory gender pay gap reporting for companies — measures that have proven effective in other countries. For a PM who claims to champion a civilisational approach to governance, the failure to meaningfully address gender economic inequality represents a significant blind spot.

🎯 PH Callout

Half of Malaysia’s population deserves more than tokenistic representation and empty rhetoric. Put women in cabinet for the photos, then ignore the structural inequality that holds women back economically. Pay transparency costs nothing to legislate. Gender budgeting is best practice globally. If you won’t do the easy things, you certainly won’t do the hard ones.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: Malaysia Refuses to Ratify ICERD — Anwar Caves to Racial Politics on International Human Rights Treaty
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim Administration / Malay-Muslim Groups

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia remains one of the few countries in the world that has not ratified the International Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Racial Discrimination (ICERD). In 2018, PH’s attempt to ratify ICERD was abandoned after mass protests led by Malay-Muslim groups who argued it would undermine Bumiputera privileges. Anwar, once a progressive leader committed to equality, has not revisited the issue since becoming PM. The “reformist” Prime Minister has accepted the status quo on racial discrimination — ICERD ratification is not on the agenda.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: ICERD ratification would commit Malaysia to eliminating racial discrimination in law and practice. Malaysia’s refusal to ratify — alongside countries like North Korea and Myanmar — places it in embarrassing company. The refusal signals to the international community that Malaysia is unwilling to subject its race-based policies to international scrutiny. For non-Bumiputera Malaysians, the refusal to ratify confirms that their rights are subordinate to racial politics.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar spent decades advocating for a “Malaysian Malaysia” — equality regardless of race. He founded the multi-racial PH coalition on the principle that all Malaysians deserve equal treatment. ICERD ratification would be the natural expression of that principle. Instead, Anwar has accepted the 2018 defeat and moved on. The man who was imprisoned partly for challenging racial politics has, as PM, decided that racial politics are too powerful to challenge. The reformist has capitulated to the very forces he built his career opposing.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Malaysia signed ICERD in 1966 but never ratified it. Under Mahathir 1.0, ratification was never considered. Under Najib, it was ignored. Under PH 1.0, ratification was announced and then abandoned after protests. Under PH 2.0, it hasn’t even been raised. Each government decides that the political cost of challenging racial discrimination is higher than the cost of maintaining it. ICERD joins the Sedition Act, the PPPA, and SOSMA on the list of reforms PH promised and abandoned.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You built a coalition on multiracial equality. Your manifesto promised a “New Malaysia” where race wouldn’t determine rights. Ratifying ICERD would be the simplest, most direct expression of that promise. Instead, you’ve accepted the 2018 defeat and moved on — just like every government before you. Either ICERD is consistent with the Malaysia you promised, or the Malaysia you promised was a lie. Which is it?

Sources: UN OHCHR | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: Road Deaths at Catastrophic Levels — Malaysia Among Deadliest Roads in ASEAN, Government's Response: 'Speed Cameras'
item.description:
Said by: MIROS / WHO Road Safety Data

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Malaysia records over 6,000 road deaths annually — approximately 16 people every day. Motorcyclists make up the largest share of fatalities. WHO data ranks Malaysia among the deadliest roads in ASEAN with a road traffic death rate significantly above the global average. The government’s primary response has been speed cameras, periodic enforcement campaigns, and awareness programmes. No structural reform of road safety infrastructure, licensing standards, or vehicle safety requirements has been implemented under PH.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: 6,000 deaths per year is a public health crisis that receives a fraction of the attention given to lesser issues. For context, Malaysia loses more people to road accidents annually than to most diseases. The economic cost — medical expenses, lost productivity, insurance claims — runs into billions. Motorcyclists, who are disproportionately B40 and young, bear the highest risk. The death toll has remained at catastrophic levels for over a decade with no meaningful decline, suggesting that current interventions are not working.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Speed cameras and enforcement campaigns are band-aid solutions. The structural causes of Malaysia’s road death crisis are well-documented: poor road design (especially rural roads without barriers), lax licensing standards (motorcycle licences are too easy to obtain), inadequate vehicle safety requirements (no mandatory ABS on motorcycles, no crash testing regime), and a culture of enforcement-then-amnesty that undermines deterrence. The government knows what works — segregated motorcycle lanes, mandatory safety equipment, stricter licensing — but implementing these requires investment and political will. Speed cameras are cheap. Structural reform is not.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Road safety has been a declared government priority under every administration since the 1990s. Under Mahathir 1.0, the Road Safety Department was established. Under Najib, the Road Safety Plan was launched. Under PH, the same plans continue with the same results: 6,000+ deaths per year. The UN Decade of Action for Road Safety (2011-2020) set targets Malaysia missed. The new decade (2021-2030) is heading the same direction. Each government announces road safety campaigns. None reduces the death toll.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Sixteen Malaysians die on the roads every day. Your response is speed cameras. The structural interventions that work — segregated motorcycle lanes, mandatory ABS, stricter licensing, road design reform — require investment and political courage you haven’t shown. Allocate a dedicated road safety infrastructure budget. Mandate motorcycle ABS. Reform the licensing system. Or keep installing speed cameras while 6,000 families lose someone every year — and pretend the problem is speed, not infrastructure.

Sources: WHO Road Safety | MIROS | FMT

item.title: Sedition Act STILL Not Repealed — PH Promised to Abolish It in 2018 and 2022, Then Used It Instead
item.description:
Said by: Lawyers for Liberty / Article 19 / Bar Council

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

The Sedition Act 1948 — a colonial-era law that PH explicitly promised to repeal in both its 2018 and 2022 election manifestos — remains fully operational under the Anwar government. Not only has it not been repealed, it is being actively used against government critics. Activist Chegubard was charged under the Act for his Forest City casino social media post.

🔥 Why It Matters

The Act criminalises speech that is ‘seditious,’ a vaguely defined term that gives authorities enormous discretion to prosecute political expression. Lawyers for Liberty has repeatedly condemned the government’s continued reliance on repressive laws. The Malaysian Bar Council has called for its repeal. International press freedom organisations have flagged its use as incompatible with democratic norms.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Alongside the Sedition Act, other repressive laws PH promised to repeal remain on the books: the Printing Presses and Publications Act 1984, the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 (SOSMA), and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act (used to prosecute online speech). Every single repressive law PH campaigned against is still available for PH to use — and PH is using them. The betrayal is complete.

🎯 PH Callout

You promised to repeal these laws. Twice. In writing. In your manifesto. You didn’t just break a promise — you weaponised the very tools you swore to destroy. When you use the Sedition Act against critics, you’re not governing — you’re doing exactly what BN did, with the added insult of having promised not to.

Sources

  • NST — Chegubard Sedition Case
  • Freedom House — Malaysia

item.title: Single Mothers in Poverty — 300,000+ Families Led by Women Living Below the Poverty Line
item.description:
Said by: Women’s Ministry / DOSM Data

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

An estimated 300,000+ single-mother households in Malaysia live at or below the poverty line, facing inadequate government support, employment discrimination, and insufficient childcare infrastructure. Single mothers are disproportionately represented among B40 households and face barriers including limited access to affordable housing, healthcare, and skills training.

🔥 Why It Matters

Single mothers and their children represent one of the most vulnerable demographics in Malaysia. Children in these households face higher risks of educational dropout, malnutrition, and intergenerational poverty. The lack of affordable childcare forces many single mothers to choose between working (and leaving children unsupervised) or staying home (and being unable to afford basic needs).

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

Government programmes for single mothers exist on paper but are fragmented, underfunded, and bureaucratically complex to access. The Bantuan Ibu Tunggal and various state-level programmes reach only a fraction of eligible recipients. Meanwhile, the government finds billions for GLC bailouts, political appointments, and civil service perks. The contrast between how Malaysia treats political elites and how it treats single mothers tells you everything about government priorities.

📜 Previous Cases

Under BN, single mother welfare was also inadequate. PH promised to do better. Under PH 1.0, minimal progress was made before the government collapsed. Under PH 2.0, single mothers remain an afterthought — occasionally mentioned in budget speeches, rarely served by meaningful policy. The gender pay gap (6-9%), lack of affordable childcare, and employment discrimination that compound single mothers’ poverty remain unaddressed.

🎯 PH Callout

You talk about ‘Madani’ values — compassion, civilisation, humanity. There are 300,000+ families led by single mothers living in poverty. These are real women and real children, not statistics. Your RM2.6 billion JAKIM budget could transform single mother welfare overnight. Your RM85.5 million J-Kom propaganda budget could fund childcare centres. But you’d rather fund religious bureaucracy and government PR than feed hungry children. That’s not Madani — that’s malicious.

Sources

  • Eurasia Review — Race-Based Economic Policies

item.title: SOSMA Still Unrepealed — PH Using the Security Law They Called 'Draconian' to Detain Without Trial
item.description:
Said by: Lawyers for Liberty / Bar Council / Amnesty International

Category: Social

Date: 2025-01-01

📌 What Happened

The Security Offences (Special Measures) Act 2012 (SOSMA), which allows detention without trial for up to 28 days and restricts judicial oversight of security cases, remains fully operational under the Anwar government. PH explicitly promised to repeal SOSMA in both its 2018 and 2022 manifestos, calling it ‘draconian’ and incompatible with democratic governance.

🔥 Why It Matters

Detention without trial is fundamentally incompatible with the rule of law. SOSMA gives the government the power to detain individuals for nearly a month without charge and without full judicial scrutiny. In the wrong hands, it’s a tool for political persecution. Every day it remains on the books is a day any Malaysian can be detained without the protections that democracy promises.

💩 Why It’s Bullshit

PH called SOSMA ‘draconian’ when BN used it. PH politicians gave speeches condemning it. Lawyers for Liberty documented its abuse. The Bar Council called for its repeal. Amnesty International flagged it annually. Now PH is in power with a supermajority, and SOSMA remains. Not amended. Not reformed. Not repealed. Unchanged and available for use.

📜 Previous Cases

Under BN, SOSMA was used against political activists. Under PH 1.0, no repeal in 22 months. Under PH 2.0, over three years, no repeal. The Sedition Act: same story. PPPA: same story. EVERY repressive law PH promised to abolish: same story. The pattern is not accidental.

🎯 PH Callout

You have the numbers. You have the mandate. You spent YEARS calling SOSMA draconian. Repeal it. You won’t — because having the power to detain without trial is convenient, even for ‘reformists.’ Your refusal proves your opposition was always about who held the power, never about the principle.

Sources

  • Freedom House — Malaysia 2024
  • Malay Mail / Amnesty — Reform Failures

item.title: Online Safety Act Passes — MCMC Gets Sweeping Censorship Powers, No Oversight
item.description:
Said by: Parliament / Fahmi Fadzil

Category: Social

Date: 2024-12-20

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2024, Parliament passed the Online Safety Act and CMA amendments giving MCMC sweeping censorship powers with penalties up to RM10 million for non-compliant platforms. The legislation empowers MCMC to order content removal, block websites, and penalise social media companies — all without independent judicial oversight. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil described it as necessary to protect children and combat cyber threats. Freedom House flagged it as a major regression in internet freedom. No independent oversight mechanism was included.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The Online Safety Act gives a government agency — MCMC, which reports to the Communications Minister — the power to determine what Malaysians can see online. There is no independent tribunal, no judicial review requirement before content removal, and no transparency mechanism for blocked content. When a government agency can remove content and penalise platforms up to RM10 million without independent oversight, every platform has an incentive to over-comply — removing borderline content preemptively rather than risking penalties. The result is censorship by incentive, not just censorship by order.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH in opposition fought against exactly these kinds of laws. The Anti-Fake News Act was PH’s exhibit A for BN’s authoritarianism — and that Act was milder than what PH has now passed. The Anti-Fake News Act had fines of up to RM500,000. The Online Safety Act has penalties of up to RM10 million. The Anti-Fake News Act targeted individual speakers. The Online Safety Act targets platforms — meaning MCMC can threaten to shut down entire services unless they comply. PH has replaced a bad law with a worse one and called it reform.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, the Anti-Fake News Act (2018), Sedition Act, and Section 233 CMA formed the internet censorship toolkit. PH repealed the Anti-Fake News Act in 2018 and promised to reform the CMA. Under PH 2.0, the CMA was strengthened (not reformed), the Online Safety Act was passed (adding new powers), and MCMC was given enforcement tools that exceed anything BN had. The censorship infrastructure PH built in three years is more comprehensive than what BN built in 60 years.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You repealed the Anti-Fake News Act because it gave the government too much power over online speech. Then you passed the Online Safety Act, which gives the government even more power with even bigger penalties and no independent oversight. You didn’t reform internet regulation — you supercharged it. Include independent judicial oversight for all content removal orders. Cap penalties at levels that don’t create incentives for over-compliance. Publish transparency reports on all MCMC content removal requests. Or accept that you’ve built the most comprehensive internet censorship framework in Malaysian history — and own it.

Sources: Freedom House | ARTICLE 19 | Malaysiakini

item.title: Bloomberg: Malaysia 'Faces Backlash Over Plan to Broaden Internet Controls'
item.description:
Said by: Bloomberg and critics

Category: Social

Date: 2024-12-04

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In December 2024, Bloomberg reported that Malaysia faced backlash over plans to tighten its grip on the internet. The government sought broader powers and penalties for internet misuse through new regulations. Rest of World reported that creators in Malaysia were self-censoring to avoid crackdowns — “The new rules make it harder to make a living,” they said. ARTICLE 19 called the Federal Court’s CMA decision reinstating “grossly offensive” provisions “a blow to freedom of expression.” Critical news websites were blocked. MCMC was given powers to penalise platforms up to RM10 million.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Malaysia’s internet was once one of the freest in the region — protected by the MSC Malaysia Bill of Guarantees that promised no internet censorship. PH is systematically dismantling that guarantee. When content creators self-censor, the chilling effect has already succeeded — no prosecution needed. The combination of website blocking, platform licensing, social media penalties, and the reinstated “grossly offensive” CMA provision creates a comprehensive censorship infrastructure that didn’t exist when PH took power.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH rode the free internet to power. Facebook, YouTube, and WhatsApp were the platforms that broke BN’s media monopoly. Malaysiakini and independent blogs were PH’s lifeline when mainstream media was controlled. Now PH is blocking websites, licensing platforms, penalising content, and creating self-censorship through regulatory threat. The MSC Bill of Guarantees — a founding promise of Malaysia’s digital economy — is dead. PH didn’t just break the promise; it buried it under multiple layers of new regulation that give MCMC more power than it ever had under BN.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, the Anti-Fake News Act was PH’s primary evidence of authoritarian internet control. PH repealed it in 2018. Under PH 2.0, the replacement regime is more comprehensive: website blocking through MCMC, platform licensing, the Online Safety Act, CMA amendments with RM10 million penalties, and self-censorship driven by regulatory uncertainty. Each individual measure is framed as protecting children, combating scams, or fighting misinformation. Collectively, they constitute a censorship apparatus that exceeds anything BN built.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Bloomberg, Rest of World, ARTICLE 19, and Freedom House all agree: you’re building an internet censorship regime. Creators are self-censoring. Websites are blocked. Platforms face RM10 million penalties. The MSC Bill of Guarantees promised no internet censorship. You’ve broken that promise more comprehensively than any previous government. Withdraw the excessive penalties. Unblock critical news sites. Protect the free internet that gave you power. Or accept that you’ve built the censorship infrastructure every future authoritarian government will thank you for.

Sources: Bloomberg | Rest of World | ARTICLE 19

item.title: Orang Asli Land Rights Violations Continue — Indigenous Communities Displaced for Logging and Plantations
item.description:
Said by: Amnesty International / Indigenous Rights Groups

Category: Social

Date: 2024-12-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Throughout 2024, Orang Asli and indigenous communities in Peninsular Malaysia continued to face systematic violations of customary land rights. Ancestral forests were cleared for logging concessions and palm oil plantations, often without free, prior, and informed consent. Amnesty International flagged indigenous rights as one of seven areas where Malaysia fell short of its human rights obligations. Orang Asli make up just 0.2% of Malaysia’s civil service despite comprising approximately 0.7% of the population — even proportional representation is denied.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Orang Asli are Malaysia’s most marginalised community. Their customary land rights — recognised by courts but systematically violated by state governments — are the foundation of their identity, livelihood, and cultural survival. When ancestral forests are cleared for plantations, Orang Asli don’t just lose land — they lose food sources, water catchments, medicinal plants, and burial grounds. The PH government, which claims to champion human rights and social justice, has done nothing to strengthen legal protections for indigenous land rights or enforce existing court rulings.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH includes politicians who have advocated for indigenous rights in opposition. Baru Bian — a prominent land rights lawyer and PH MP — has fought indigenous land cases for decades. Yet in government, PH has not introduced legislation to codify customary land rights, has not enforced existing court rulings protecting indigenous territories, and has not increased Orang Asli representation in the civil service or decision-making bodies. The 0.2% civil service representation figure means Orang Asli are virtually invisible in the government that claims to speak for all Malaysians.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Indigenous land dispossession has been ongoing since independence. Under BN, logging concessions were issued to politically connected companies with no consultation. Under PH 1.0, a brief moratorium on certain concessions was announced before the Sheraton Move. Under PH 2.0, the pattern has resumed. Court rulings recognising customary land rights — including landmark Federal Court decisions — are routinely ignored by state governments. The legal framework exists to protect indigenous rights. The political will to enforce it does not.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Orang Asli land is still being taken. Court rulings are still being ignored. Your civil service has 0.2% Orang Asli representation. Amnesty flagged it. Indigenous rights groups are documenting it. And your government is silent. Codify customary land rights in federal legislation. Enforce existing court rulings. Increase indigenous representation in government. Or keep talking about “Madani civilisation” while Malaysia’s original inhabitants watch their ancestral forests being turned into palm oil plantations — under your watch.

Sources: Amnesty International | Survival International | Malaysiakini

item.title: GISBH Child Abuse Scandal — Amirudin Says Abuses Were 'Concealed by Shifting to Business'
item.description:
Said by: Amirudin Shari, Selangor Menteri Besar

Category: Social

Date: 2024-09-15

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Following the explosive GISBH (Global Ikhwan Services and Business Holdings) scandal involving sexual and physical abuse of children in welfare homes, Amirudin stated that the organisation had ‘concealed abuses by shifting to business’ — suggesting that GISBH’s transition from the banned Al-Arqam movement into a legitimate business empire allowed it to hide criminal activity. Hundreds of children were rescued from GISBH-linked premises across Malaysia, with many found in Selangor.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: GISBH operated openly in Selangor for years. Its premises were known. Its business operations were visible. Its welfare homes housed hundreds of children. The Selangor state government — which oversees local authority licensing, land use, welfare inspections, and Islamic affairs — either failed to detect the abuse or failed to act on it. Amirudin’s explanation that GISBH ‘concealed’ itself by becoming a business is an admission that his state government was unable to monitor organisations operating openly within its borders.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: You don’t get to govern Selangor for six years, have a massive child abuse ring operating in your state, and then say ’they hid it well.’ State welfare departments are supposed to inspect children’s homes. Local authorities issue business licences. JAIS oversees Islamic organisations. If all these agencies missed what was happening in GISBH homes, that’s not concealment — that’s institutional failure. And institutional failure in Selangor is Amirudin’s responsibility.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Al-Arqam was banned in 1994 — thirty years ago. Its transformation into GISBH was well-documented. Journalists and activists had raised concerns about the organisation for years. The pattern of authorities ignoring well-documented abuses until they explode into public scandal is a Malaysian governance tradition that PH was supposed to end.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Children were abused in welfare homes in your state, under your governance, by an organisation that has been on the radar for thirty years. Your excuse is that they ‘concealed’ themselves by becoming a business. That is not an explanation — it is a confession that your welfare inspection, licensing, and monitoring systems are broken. Fix them, or the next GISBH is already operating in Selangor while you make speeches about development.

Sources: Yahoo News

item.title: Hannah Yeoh Fangirls Over Thai Shuttler Who Just Eliminated Malaysia's Lee Zii Jia — Tone-Deaf at the Olympics
item.description:
Said by: Hannah Yeoh, Youth and Sports Minister (then)

Category: Social

Date: 2024-08-07

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In August 2024 at the Paris Olympics, Malaysian shuttler Lee Zii Jia was knocked out in the men’s singles semi-final by Thailand’s Kunlavut Vitidsarn — a devastating loss for Malaysian fans hoping for an Olympic medal. Days later, Youth and Sports Minister Hannah Yeoh posted selfies with Kunlavut on Instagram, praising him. SCMP reported she was “smashed on social media for ‘fangirl’ post.” Mothership called it “insensitive.” Yeoh defended her post, saying Malaysia is “not a sore loser” and that it was her duty to “instil a culture of healthy competition.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Timing matters in politics. A Sports Minister’s job at the Olympics is to support the national team. Taking selfies with the athlete who just crushed your country’s medal hopes — and posting them on social media — shows a fundamental lack of political instinct. Nobody would have objected if Yeoh had congratulated Kunlavut privately or weeks later. The issue is posting fangirl selfies while the wound was still fresh, while millions of Malaysians were still processing the disappointment. Read the room.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Yeoh’s defence — promoting sportsmanship — is a classic politician’s deflection. Nobody accused her of lacking sportsmanship. They accused her of lacking sensitivity. There’s a difference. Imagine a Japanese Sports Minister posting selfies with an American swimmer who just eliminated Japan’s favourite — it wouldn’t happen, because competent politicians understand that the public’s emotions matter during the Olympics. Yeoh’s tone-deafness isn’t new. It’s a pattern of a politician who confuses personal branding with ministerial responsibilities.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: This wasn’t Yeoh’s only Olympics controversy. The Malaysian Olympic attire design for Paris 2024 also sparked public criticism, with many questioning why the national kit looked uninspired. Yeoh, as Sports Minister, bore responsibility for the overall presentation of Team Malaysia. Instead of a proud Olympic moment, Malaysia got two controversies. Yeoh’s tenure as Sports Minister was marked by a series of tone-deaf moments that suggested disconnection from public sentiment rather than deliberate insensitivity.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You sent Hannah Yeoh to Paris to support Malaysia’s Olympic athletes. She came back with selfies with the guy who beat our best shuttler. The Sports Minister’s job is to champion Malaysian sport, not audition for a Thai fan club. The fact that she couldn’t understand why Malaysians were upset tells you everything about how disconnected PH ministers are from public sentiment. Political instinct isn’t optional — it’s the job.

Sources: SCMP/Straits Times | MalaysiaNow | Mothership | The Vibes

item.title: Fahmi Fadzil Forces Social Media Licensing on Platforms — Critics Call It PH's Version of Internet Censorship
item.description:
Said by: Fahmi Fadzil, Communications Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2024-07-28

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 28 July 2024, the PH government announced that social media platforms with more than 8 million users in Malaysia would be required to apply for a regulatory licence from MCMC, effective 1 August. Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil said the measure was needed to combat cyber offences including online scams, child exploitation, and deepfakes. By late 2025, Fahmi was threatening legal action against non-compliant platforms. In January 2026, MCMC temporarily blocked X’s Grok AI feature over deepfake concerns. ARTICLE 19 called on Malaysia to “halt hasty imposition of mandatory electronic verification,” warning about implications for privacy and free speech.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Social media licensing gives the government a lever to pressure platforms into compliance with content removal requests. While the stated goals — combating scams, protecting children — are legitimate, the mechanism creates obvious censorship risks. When a government can threaten to revoke a platform’s operating licence, it has enormous power over what content stays up and what comes down. In a country with a history of using the Communications and Multimedia Act to prosecute critics, handing MCMC more regulatory power over social media is a loaded gun.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH rode social media to power. Facebook, Twitter, YouTube, and WhatsApp were the platforms that allowed PH to circumvent BN’s control of mainstream media. Without social media, the 1MDB scandal might never have reached the Malaysian public at the scale it did. Now in power, PH is licensing the same platforms, threatening legal action against those that don’t comply, and blocking AI features. The “child safety” framing is clever but transparent — the licensing framework applies to all content, not just child safety. PH repealed the Anti-Fake News Act and called themselves defenders of internet freedom. Then they introduced a licensing regime that gives MCMC even more power than the Act they repealed.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, the Anti-Fake News Act 2018 was PH’s exhibit A for authoritarian internet control. PH repealed it in 2018. Now PH has replaced it with something arguably more powerful: a licensing regime that gives MCMC ongoing regulatory leverage over platforms, rather than just post-hoc prosecution. PH also opposed MCMC’s use of Section 233 of the CMA to prosecute critics under BN. Under PH, the same section continues to be used, and platforms themselves can now be held liable through the licensing framework.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You repealed the Anti-Fake News Act and called yourselves defenders of internet freedom. Then you introduced a licensing regime that gives MCMC even more power over social media than BN ever had. You’re licensing the internet while claiming to protect it. The child safety argument is legitimate — but the licensing framework goes far beyond child safety. Be honest about what you’re building: a regulatory tool that can pressure platforms into compliance with government content demands. That’s not reform. That’s BN’s internet controls with a PH label.

Sources: Reuters | ARTICLE 19 | Malay Mail

item.title: MCMC Blocks Critical News Sites — MalaysiaNow, Asia Sentinel, UtusanTV Censored
item.description:
Said by: Freedom House Report 2024

Category: Social

Date: 2024-06-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In mid-2024, MCMC blocked news outlets critical of the government including MalaysiaNow, UtusanTV, TV Pertiwi, and Asia Sentinel. A new Code of Ethics effectively grants or bars media access based on compliance — creating a de facto licensing system for journalism. A man was sentenced to 6 months for a Facebook post insulting a Sultan. Freedom House’s 2024 report documented Malaysia’s internet freedom as declining under PH, flagging the blocking of news sites and the expansion of MCMC’s content moderation powers as key concerns.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Blocking entire news outlets — not individual articles, but entire publications — is authoritarian behaviour regardless of which party does it. MalaysiaNow, Asia Sentinel, and UtusanTV were blocked because they published content the government found inconvenient, not because they were publishing illegal material. When the government controls which news outlets Malaysians can access, it controls the information environment. Freedom House explicitly flagged this as a decline in internet freedom — under a government that promised to protect it.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar once relied on independent media to survive politically. Without Malaysiakini, Sarawak Report, and international outlets covering 1MDB, his political comeback would not have been possible. Now his government is blocking news sites that publish critical coverage. The Code of Ethics creates a carrot-and-stick system where compliant media gets access and critical media gets blocked. This is not regulation — it is state management of the press. The irony of a former political prisoner censoring the press that helped free him is beyond parody.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, Sarawak Report was blocked for its 1MDB coverage. The Malaysian Insider was shut down. The Printing Presses and Publications Act required annual licences for print media. PH condemned all of this as authoritarian. Under PH, the approach is subtler but equally effective: instead of banning publications through licensing, you block them through MCMC and control access through the Code of Ethics. The end result is identical — the government decides which media the public can consume.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You survived because independent media told your story when the government wanted to silence you. Now you’re the government, and you’re silencing independent media. Unblock MalaysiaNow, Asia Sentinel, and UtusanTV. Withdraw the Code of Ethics that functions as a gatekeeping mechanism. Let journalists do their jobs without government approval. Or keep blocking the press while claiming to protect press freedom — and hope Malaysians don’t notice the contradiction.

Sources: Freedom House

item.title: Police Excessive Force at Protests — Reformasi Leader Now Uses Riot Police Against His Own Supporters
item.description:
Said by: Lawyers for Liberty / Suaram / Bersih

Category: Social

Date: 2024-06-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: Throughout 2024, multiple incidents of police using excessive force and pre-emptive arrests against peaceful protesters were documented under the Anwar government. Lawyers for Liberty, Suaram, and Bersih all issued condemnations. Police conducted home visits to warn potential rally participants, arrested organisers before events, and deployed disproportionate force at demonstrations. The Peaceful Assembly Act — which PH had promised to reform — was used to justify suppression of the same civil liberties PH once championed.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Anwar Ibrahim built his entire political career on street protests. The Reformasi movement began as a mass protest. Bersih rallies were PH’s political oxygen. The right to peaceful assembly was the foundational civil liberty that made PH’s rise to power possible. Now PH uses the same PDRM apparatus — the same riot police, the same PAA provisions, the same intimidation tactics — to suppress protesters demanding the reforms PH promised. The transformation from protest movement to protest suppressor took less than two years.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH promised to reform the Peaceful Assembly Act to remove prior notification requirements and police veto powers. Instead, police are using the Act more aggressively than under BN for equivalent events. Lawyers for Liberty documented cases where police cited Nik Nazmi’s own 2013 case law — law that PKR fought and won — to justify suppressing assembly rights under PH’s government. The party that created the legal precedent for protest rights is now ignoring that precedent. This isn’t policy drift — it’s deliberate repression.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, police crackdowns on Bersih rallies were front-page international news. Tear gas at Bersih 2.0 (2011), water cannons at Bersih 3.0 (2012), and mass arrests at subsequent rallies were cited by HRW, Amnesty, and RSF as evidence of Malaysia’s authoritarian governance. PH used every single crackdown to build sympathy, recruit supporters, and fundraise. The international community recognised PH as a democratic movement fighting for fundamental freedoms. Now the same international community is documenting PH doing exactly what BN did.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You were the protest movement. Your leader was jailed for it. Your MPs were charged for it. Your supporters were tear-gassed for it. And now you’re doing it to others. Reform the Peaceful Assembly Act as promised. Issue a directive prohibiting pre-emptive arrests and home visits for rally participants. Hold PDRM accountable for excessive force. Or accept that Reformasi ended the day you started using riot police against the same people who marched for your freedom.

Sources: FMT | Suaram | Lawyers for Liberty

item.title: Press Cards as Weapons — New Code of Ethics Lets Government Decide Who Is a 'Real' Journalist
item.description:
Said by: Freedom House / Journalists’ Union

Category: Social

Date: 2024-06-01

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In mid-2024, the government released an updated Code of Ethics for Journalists that effectively creates a de facto licensing system for journalism in Malaysia. Media access to government events, press conferences, and official sources is increasingly dependent on compliance with the code — meaning outlets that publish critical reporting risk losing access. Freedom House documented this as a form of soft censorship. Self-censorship becomes the mechanism: critical stories risk loss of access, advertising boycotts from government-linked companies, or MCMC investigations.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: When the government decides who qualifies as a “real” journalist, it controls the information environment without needing to formally censor anyone. The Code of Ethics doesn’t ban publications — it gates access. Journalists who comply get press cards, access to ministers, and invitations to official events. Journalists who don’t comply lose all of this. The result is soft censorship: editors learn which stories cost them access, and they adjust accordingly. No law is broken. No outlet is formally banned. The press is simply managed.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH campaigned explicitly on press freedom. Anwar, a political prisoner whose case was partly championed by press freedom organisations worldwide, should understand better than anyone the importance of a free press. Instead, his government has created a code of ethics that functions as a gatekeeping mechanism — deciding who gets to be a journalist and who doesn’t, based on compliance with government-set standards. Every journalist who self-censors to keep their access is a journalist PH has silenced without the bad optics of a formal ban. Subtle censorship is still censorship.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, the Printing Presses and Publications Act required annual licences for print media — direct censorship that PH promised to repeal. They didn’t repeal it. Under PH, the approach is subtler but equally effective: instead of requiring licences for publications, you require compliance for access. The Sedition Act, the PPPA, and now the Code of Ethics form a layered system of media control that is more sophisticated than BN’s but achieves the same outcome — a press that knows the boundaries and stays within them.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: A free press protects democracy. You know this — you were a political prisoner partly rescued by press freedom. Now you’ve created a system where journalists need government approval to be “real” journalists. Withdraw the Code of Ethics as a gatekeeping mechanism. Guarantee access to all accredited journalists regardless of editorial line. Repeal the PPPA as promised. Or keep managing the press through access control — and accept that your press freedom record is indistinguishable from BN’s.

Sources: Freedom House | SCMP

item.title: Press Freedom Plunges 34 Spots to 107th — 'Difficult Situation' Under PH
item.description:
Said by: Reporters Without Borders (RSF)

Category: Social

Date: 2024-05-03

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In May 2024, Reporters Without Borders (RSF) released the 2024 World Press Freedom Index, in which Malaysia plunged 34 places — from 73rd to 107th — under the PH government. RSF classified Malaysia as a “difficult situation” for press freedom. The collapse was driven by sedition charges against journalists and activists, MCMC content blocking, social media licensing requirements, and the arrest of FMT journalist Rex Tan. Even after a partial recovery to 88th in 2025, Malaysia still trails its 2023 ranking under PH.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: A 34-place drop in press freedom under a government that promised media reform is not a statistical fluctuation — it is an indictment. PH’s 2022 manifesto included commitments to repeal the Sedition Act, reform the Printing Presses and Publications Act, and protect press freedom. Instead, under PH, journalists have been arrested, news sites blocked, and a social media licensing regime introduced. RSF’s “difficult situation” classification places Malaysia alongside countries PH claims to be morally superior to. The ranking reflects not just specific incidents but a systemic deterioration in the media environment.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s response to the ranking drop was to blame “inherited problems” and promise improvement. But the specific incidents RSF cited — Chegubard’s sedition charge, Rex Tan’s arrest, MCMC blocking MalaysiaNow and Asia Sentinel — all occurred under PH’s watch. These are not inherited problems; they are PH-created problems. The government that promised to protect journalists is now prosecuting them. The government that promised to unblock websites is now blocking more. Every press freedom commitment has been reversed.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under Najib, Malaysia’s press freedom ranking hovered around 140th — driven by 1MDB-related media suppression, the blocking of Sarawak Report, and sedition charges against editors. PH 1.0 achieved a dramatic improvement to 101st in 2019. PH 2.0 initially maintained the improvement before the 2024 collapse. The trajectory is clear: PH delivers press freedom gains in opposition and early government, then reverts to suppression once media scrutiny becomes inconvenient. The 34-place drop happened in a single year — suggesting a deliberate policy shift, not gradual decline.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You dropped 34 places in press freedom in a single year. RSF calls Malaysia a “difficult situation.” Your manifesto promised media freedom. Your government delivered media suppression. Unblock MalaysiaNow and Asia Sentinel. Drop charges against journalists. Repeal the Sedition Act as promised. Withdraw the social media licensing regime. Or keep falling in the rankings — and stop pretending Malaysia is a democracy when the world’s press freedom watchdog classifies it as difficult.

Sources: RSF | Malaysiakini | FMT

item.title: Chegubard Charged with SEDITION for Reporting Forest City Casino Claims — Reformasi's Free Speech Promise Dies
item.description:
Said by: Johor Sessions Court / Badrul Hisham (Chegubard)

Category: Social

Date: 2024-04-30

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 30 April 2024, activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin (Chegubard) was charged under Malaysia’s colonial-era Sedition Act for a social media post about casino plans at Forest City in Johor. The Sedition Act is the same law PH promised to repeal during its 2018 and 2022 election campaigns. As of March 2026, Chegubard’s appeal is still pending at the Johor Bahru Sessions Court. Instead of repealing the Sedition Act, the Anwar government is now weaponising it against critics — the exact same playbook used by BN against Anwar himself in the 1990s.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Every repressive law PH promised to abolish — the Sedition Act, the Printing Presses and Publications Act, SOSMA, Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act — is still on the books and being actively used by PH. Press freedom organisations condemned the prosecution as a chilling signal to journalists and activists: in Anwar’s Malaysia, questioning powerful interests — especially those connected to the Forest City special economic zone — can land you in court. The message is clear: free speech ends where powerful business interests begin.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: Anwar Ibrahim was himself charged under the Sedition Act. He knows what it feels like to face prosecution for speech. He built an entire political movement — Reformasi — on the injustice of being silenced by the state. Now his government is using the same law against a man who reported on casino plans at a development the government is actively promoting. The Sedition Act wasn’t just left unrepealed — it was expanded, with over 90 sedition-related arrests under PH. The man who was once a victim of these laws is now wielding them.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, the Sedition Act was used against opposition politicians, academics, lawyers, and journalists. Anwar, Karpal Singh, Azmi Sharom, and dozens of others were charged or investigated. PH condemned every single prosecution as authoritarian overreach. Under PH 2.0, the same law has been used against Chegubard, social media commentators, and critics of government policy. The only difference is the name on the charge sheet — the law, the process, and the chilling effect are identical.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You were jailed under these laws. You know what they feel like. You promised to repeal them. And now you’re using them against the same kind of activists who marched for your freedom. Repeal the Sedition Act — as you promised in 2018 and 2022. Drop the charges against Chegubard. Or admit that Reformasi was never about free speech — it was about freeing one man, not the system.

Sources: NST | SCMP

item.title: Police Threaten Bersih Rally Participants — PH Government Uses Same Tactics It Once Condemned
item.description:
Said by: Bersih / Lawyers for Liberty

Category: Social

Date: 2024-02-26

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In February 2024, the PH government sent police warnings that Bersih’s planned demonstration was illegal. Officers visited participants’ homes and issued warnings ahead of the rally. Lawyers for Liberty (LFL) condemned it as “a betrayal by PH,” citing the government’s use of the same intimidation tactics PH had once fought against. CSIS noted that civil society groups are “torn between their historical alliance with PH and the government’s failure to implement its reform agenda.” Bersih — the electoral reform coalition that was PH’s closest ally during the BN era — was now being treated as a threat.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Bersih’s rallies were the oxygen of PH’s rise to power. The Bersih 2.0 through 5.0 rallies built the mass movement that eventually toppled BN. PH leaders didn’t just support these rallies — they marched in them, were tear-gassed at them, and used them to build their democratic credentials. Now the same government is sending police to people’s homes to warn them against attending a Bersih rally. The message is unmistakable: freedom of assembly is only acceptable when it serves PH’s interests.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: LFL’s condemnation specifically cited a 2014 Court of Appeal ruling — from PKR’s own Nik Nazmi’s case — that struck down restrictive provisions of the Peaceful Assembly Act. PH’s own legal victory against protest suppression is now being ignored by PH’s own government. Nik Nazmi, who was literally charged under the PAA for organising a protest in 2013, was a PH minister when police threatened Bersih participants. The hypocrisy is not subtle — it is the defining feature of PH’s approach to civil liberties: demand them in opposition, suppress them in government.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: Under BN, police intimidation of rally participants was standard practice. Home visits, phone calls, and social media monitoring were used to discourage attendance at Bersih rallies. PH condemned these tactics as authoritarian. Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, and every Malaysian civil liberties organisation documented BN’s suppression of assembly rights. Now the same organisations are documenting the same tactics under PH — and the same civil society groups that once marched with PH are being targeted by PH.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: You marched with Bersih. You were tear-gassed with Bersih. You built your movement on the right to peaceful assembly. Now you’re sending police to people’s homes to warn them against attending a Bersih rally. Lawyers for Liberty calls it a betrayal. CSIS says civil society is torn. The rakyat who marched for you are now being warned against marching for accountability. Guarantee the right to peaceful assembly without police intimidation. Or admit that freedom of assembly was only useful when you were the ones assembling.

Sources: FMT | CSIS | Malaysiakini

item.title: HRW 2024: 'Reform Agenda Stalled' — Government Failed to Uphold Pledges in First Year
item.description:
Said by: Human Rights Watch

Category: Social

Date: 2024-01-11

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: In January 2024, Human Rights Watch released its 2024 World Report, delivering a damning verdict on Anwar Ibrahim’s first year in office: “Reform Agenda Stalled.” HRW stated that Anwar had “largely failed to uphold his pledges to address repression and corruption” and called on the government to “recommit to a rights-respecting agenda by turning its sidelined campaign promises into action.” The report documented failures across freedom of expression, assembly, media freedom, and institutional reform. A year later, HRW’s 2025 assessment was even harsher: “Rights Backslide.”

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: Two consecutive years of HRW condemnation is not a bump in the road — it is a trajectory. Year 1: stalled. Year 2: backslide. Year 3: expanded internet controls, 90+ sedition arrests, and journalist arrests. When the world’s most prominent human rights organisation says your reform agenda has stalled — and then says it’s actively going backwards — the problem is not perception. It is policy. PH was elected on a reform mandate. The international human rights community is documenting, in real time, the abandonment of that mandate.

💩 WHY IT’S BULLSHIT: PH’s response to HRW’s assessment was predictable: “They don’t understand our context.” “Coalition constraints.” “We need time.” But HRW gave PH a year before issuing the “stalled” verdict. Then another year before escalating to “backslide.” The organisation is not being impatient — it is documenting a government that promised reform and delivered regression. The Sedition Act was not just left on the books — it was expanded. Media freedom was not just neglected — journalists were arrested. Assembly rights were not just unprotected — Bersih rallies were suppressed. This is not stalling. This is reversal.

📜 PREVIOUS CASES: HRW has criticised every Malaysian government. Under Najib, the assessments focused on 1MDB, repressive laws, and crackdowns on civil society. Under Muhyiddin and Ismail Sabri, the focus was pandemic-era restrictions and democratic backsliding. But PH was supposed to be different — it was the government that HRW’s recommendations aligned with. When the human rights organisation that should be your natural ally says you’ve stalled and backslid, the problem is not the critic. It’s the government.

🎯 PH CALLOUT: Human Rights Watch gave you a year. Then another year. The verdict got worse, not better. “Stalled” became “backslide.” If the trajectory continues, Year 4 will be “regression.” Implement the reforms HRW identified: repeal the Sedition Act, strengthen media freedom protections, guarantee assembly rights, and restore MACC independence. Or keep dismissing international criticism as “not understanding context” — while the context is that you’re doing exactly what BN did, with better English.

Sources: HRW

item.title: PADU Database Launches as Privacy Nightmare — Anyone With Your IC Number Can Access Your Data
item.description:
Said by: Anwar Ibrahim, Prime Minister

Category: Social

Date: 2024-01-02

📌 WHAT HAPPENED: On 2 January 2024, Anwar launched the Central Database Hub (PADU) — a system meant to revolutionise subsidy delivery by targeting aid to those who need it. Over 233,000 users registered on launch day. Within 48 hours, security researchers discovered the authentication was laughably weak: anyone armed with another person’s IC number and home postcode could register and access their data. CybersecurityAsia called it a “privacy nightmare.” Sarawak halted its participation entirely in March 2024, citing concerns over state autonomy and citizens’ personal data. By mid-2025, PADU was quietly sidelined — the RON95 subsidy reform bypassed it entirely, using JPJ vehicle registration data instead.

🔥 WHY IT MATTERS: The government launched a national database with authentication so weak it was effectively open access.
